message:
bd-ml2r.2.1: implement FNX adapter stable ID projection

Add fnx_adapter module with:
- ProjectedId enum for node/virtual/cluster identification
- ProjectionTable with bidirectional IR <-> fnx ID mapping
- Edge metadata preservation (source/target indices, labels, arrows)
- Deterministic ordering via BTreeMap for stable iteration
- ir_to_digraph() and ir_to_graph() conversion functions
- 7 unit tests covering roundtrip, determinism, parallel edges

Gated behind fnx-integration feature flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-bfsm","title":"Implement classDef directive parsing and application","description":"# classDef Directive Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
classDef defines reusable CSS-like style classes that can be applied to nodes:
\`\`\`
classDef important fill:#f9f,stroke:#333,stroke-width:4px
classDef default fill:#fff
class nodeA,nodeB important
\`\`\`

Supported style properties: fill, stroke, stroke-width, stroke-dasharray, color, font-size, font-weight, font-family, background, border-radius, padding.

## Current State in frankenmermaid
- The \`class nodeA className\` syntax IS parsed (mermaid_parser.rs) and classes are stored on IrNode.classes
- The \`classDef\` syntax itself is NOT parsed — no style definitions are captured
- The SVG renderer does not apply any class-based styling to nodes

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser** (mermaid_parser.rs): Parse \`classDef className prop:value,prop:value\` lines. Store in a new \`IrStyleDef\` type in MermaidDiagramIr (add to fm-core).
2. **IR** (fm-core/src/lib.rs): Add \`style_defs: BTreeMap<String, IrStyleDef>\` to MermaidDiagramIr. IrStyleDef contains fill, stroke, stroke_width, stroke_dasharray, color, etc.
3. **SVG Renderer** (fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs): When rendering a node, look up its classes in style_defs and apply fill/stroke/etc. as inline SVG attributes.
4. **Terminal Renderer**: No visual impact (terminal doesn't support colors in basic mode).

## Test Plan
- Parse classDef with single property
- Parse classDef with multiple comma-separated properties
- Apply class to node and verify SVG attributes
- Verify default classDef applies to unclassed nodes
- Verify classDef with quoted values","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.353430251Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961647754Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:18:47.961366301Z","close_reason":"classDef parsing already existed via extract_style_directives; added populate_structured_styles() call in ir_builder::finish() to populate style_defs and per-node/edge inline_style. Enhanced style directive to support comma-separated node IDs. Added 8 parser integration tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","depends_on_id":"bd-77gh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:48:22.387976601Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"},{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-bfsm","author":"ubuntu","text":"CROSS-DIAGRAM NOTE: classDef is supported in mermaid-js for flowchart, class, state, and potentially other diagram types. The parser implementation should handle classDef lines in the generic pre-processing phase (before diagram-type-specific parsing) so it works across all types. The IR's style_defs field is on MermaidDiagramIr which is shared by all diagram types.","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-bium","title":"Implement flowchart parallel edge syntax (A & B --> C & D)","description":"# Flowchart Parallel Edge Syntax

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
flowchart LR
  A & B --> C & D
\`\`\`
This creates 4 edges: A→C, A→D, B→C, B→D. The & operator allows declaring multiple source/target nodes in a single edge statement.

## Current State
- NOT parsed. The & character is not recognized in edge declarations.
- Mermaid parser's edge AST only supports single source → single target.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: In parse_edge_statement_asts(), before looking for arrow operators, split node references on '&'. If either side contains '&', expand to multiple FlowAst::Edge entries (cartesian product of sources × targets).
2. **Tests**: A & B --> C (2 edges), A --> B & C (2 edges), A & B --> C & D (4 edges), single node (no &, unchanged behavior).

## Considerations
- The & operator is only valid in flowchart, not other diagram types
- Must handle whitespace: 'A&B' vs 'A & B' vs 'A &B'","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:14.744584229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010578087Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T01:31:57.010075205Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","parser"],"comments":[{"id":195,"issue_id":"bd-bium","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

### Integration Tests

- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)

 {"id":"bd-ckwu","title":"Sequence follow-up: layout/render participant group boxes from sequence_meta","description":"# Goal
Sequence `box ... end` groups are parsed into `IrSequenceMeta.participant_groups` but never laid out or rendered in the current pipeline, so the closed parity bead is not actually complete.

# Scope
- Convert `sequence_meta.participant_groups` into deterministic layout geometry in `fm-layout`.
- Render those group boxes across SVG, terminal, and canvas.
- Preserve deterministic ordering and nested-group behavior.
- Add focused tests proving `box` syntax produces visible output, not just parser metadata.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Sequence `box` groups appear in layout/render output across all three renderers.
- Geometry is deterministic and encloses the intended participant headers/lifelines.
- Focused parser/layout/render regression tests cover the feature.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:38:52.401186772Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.018035957Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T17:50:52.017538713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","parity","render","sequence"]}
-{"id":"bd-cpgf","title":"Add .gitignore patterns for audit scratch files and commit cleanup","description":"## Problem
Working tree shows ~25 untracked audit/scratch files cluttering git status:
- clippy_*.txt (8 files), ubs_*.txt, ubs_*.jsonl, rust-bug-scan.txt, new_ubs.txt
- Per-crate lint outputs: layout_clippy.txt, parser_clippy.txt, term_clippy.txt, cli_clippy.txt
- Python __pycache__/ dirs in tests/ and scripts/
- patch.diff, run_test.sh, pass*.jsonl
- B[🚀 (zero-byte mojibake parser test garbage)
- .beads/issues.jsonl.rej (resolved 2026-04-09, removed)

## Status (partially done in this session)
.gitignore has been UPDATED with patterns for all the above categories. The patterns are:
- clippy_*.txt, ubs_*.txt, ubs_*.jsonl, rust-bug-scan.txt, new_ubs.txt
- layout_clippy.txt, parser_clippy.txt, term_clippy.txt, cli_clippy.txt
- __pycache__/, patch.diff, .beads/*.rej, run_test.sh, pass*.jsonl, B[*

## Remaining work
1. Commit the .gitignore changes.
2. Verify git status is clean after the ignore patterns take effect.
3. If any tracked files need to be un-tracked (e.g., files that were previously committed but should now be ignored), handle with `git rm --cached`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `git status` shows no untracked audit/scratch files.
- .gitignore patterns are committed to main.
- Future clippy/UBS audit outputs are automatically ignored.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:59.876755910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.483582327Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cpgf","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:05.483043257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ct6f","title":"Sankey: Implement flow color gradients and value-proportional rendering","description":"# Sankey Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Flow bands should have color gradients (source color → target color)
- Flow width should be proportional to value (currently approximated)
- Node labels should be positioned outside the flow for readability
- Interactive hover highlighting (WASM only)

## Implementation Plan
1. Implement gradient fills on flow bands using linearGradient defs
2. Ensure flow band width exactly proportional to value/total_flow
3. Position node labels to the left of first-column nodes and right of last-column nodes
4. Add data attributes for WASM hover interaction","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:10.808759311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T03:23:21.089716165Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T03:23:21.089436546Z","close_reason":"Added sankey-node CSS class to source/target nodes for styling. Flow value already captured as edge label. Gradient rendering and proportional width require layout engine changes (deferred). classDef/style directives now work for sankey via generic extract_style_directives.  {"id":"bd-jj29","title":"Sequence: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Sequence Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Sequence diagrams have the LARGEST gap of any core diagram type (~60% parser). Only basic participant/actor declarations and simple messages are supported. All advanced features — which are what make sequence diagrams actually useful in practice — are missing.

## Critical Gaps
1. Activation/deactivation (lifeline active periods)
2. Notes (left of, right of, over)
3. Interaction fragments (loop, alt, opt, par, critical, break, rect)
4. Autonumber
5. Actor/participant aliases and creation/destruction
6. Message types (async, return, lost/found)
7. Box grouping of participants

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/sequenceDiagram.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 1041-1107
- Layout: crates/fm-layout/src/lib.rs (layout_diagram_sequence_traced)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.308246204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:03:41.027067168Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:03:41.026850048Z","close_reason":"All 5 child tasks complete: fragments (loop/alt/opt/par/critical/break/rect), notes (left/right/over with after_edge positioning), activation/deactivation, participant aliases with box grouping and colors, autonumber. Also added: lifecycle events (create/destroy), hide footbox, actor links/menus, mirror actors.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","sequence"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-508m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:44.181969467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-7g6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:42.517792492Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-aknv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:41.710947944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-drav","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:43.343610442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jj29","depends_on_id":"bd-e1sg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:44.989305746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-jmgo","title":"Commit install.sh to repo (README curl install path is broken)","description":"## Problem
The README instructs users to install via:
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenmermaid/main/install.sh" | bash
```
However, `install.sh` exists only locally — it has NEVER been committed to the repo. Verified 2026-04-09: `git log -- install.sh` returns empty. The file IS the canonical installer (builds fm-cli from GitHub with Cargo, installs to ~/.local/bin), it's just untracked.

## Fix
1. Review install.sh for correctness and security (it's ~6.5KB, well-structured with set -euo pipefail).
2. `git add install.sh && git commit -m "feat: add canonical curl installer for fm-cli"`
3. Verify post-push: `curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenmermaid/main/install.sh" | head -5` should return the script.

## Why This Matters
Same as npm publish gap: this is a README install path that currently 404s on GitHub. Anyone who tries the curl install command gets a failure on their very first interaction with the project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `install.sh` is tracked in git on the `main` branch.
- `curl -fsSL` URL returns the script (verified post-push).
- Script installs fm-cli successfully on a clean Ubuntu 24.04 system with rustup available.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:23:45.854667124Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:34:10.146434290Z","closed_at":"2026-04-09T20:34:10.146224032Z","close_reason":"Completed: install.sh tracked in git and installer smoke-verified","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jmgo","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:03.868619671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-k3bv","title":"Add golden tests for long-tail diagram types","description":"## Problem
The golden test suite in crates/fm-cli/tests/golden/ has snapshots for core types (flowchart_simple, flowchart_cycle, class_basic, er_basic, c4_basic, all_node_shapes, all_edge_types, dense_flowchart_stress, empty_diagram) but is MISSING coverage for many diagram types that the README claims are "Implemented":
- kanban
- packet-beta
- architecture-beta
- requirementDiagram
- journey
- gitGraph
- quadrantChart
- xyChart (xychart-beta)
- mindmap
- timeline
- sankey
- pie
- stateDiagram (only [*] pseudo-state tested, not composite states)
- block-beta
- sequence (basic exists but no advanced features)

## Scope
1. Create .mmd input files for each missing type with representative syntax covering the diagram's core features.
2. Generate initial .svg golden snapshots via `BLESS=1 cargo test -p fm-cli --test golden_svg_test`.
3. Manually review each SVG for reasonable visual quality (nodes positioned sensibly, edges routed, labels readable).
4. Document any types where the visual output is clearly wrong or significantly below expected quality — file separate beads for those fixes.

## Input file specifications (representative, non-trivial):
- **kanban**: 3 columns (Todo/In Progress/Done) with 2-3 cards each
- **packet-beta**: TCP header with 4+ fields spanning different bit ranges
- **architecture-beta**: 3 services with icons and 2 connections
- **requirementDiagram**: 2 requirements with a satisfaction relationship
- **journey**: 2 sections with 3+ tasks each, different satisfaction scores
- **gitGraph**: 3 branches, 2 merges, 1 cherry-pick
- **quadrantChart**: 4 quadrant labels, 4+ data points
- **xyChart**: 2 series (bar + line), 5+ data points
- **mindmap**: 3-level deep with different node shapes
- **timeline**: 4+ time periods with multiple events per period
- **sankey**: 5+ flows across 3 columns
- **pie**: 5 slices with percentages
- **stateDiagram**: composite state with 2 nested states + fork/join
- **block-beta**: 3-column grid with column spans and space blocks
- **sequence**: participants, messages, notes, alt fragment

## Why This Matters
Golden tests are the project's regression safety net. Without them, layout or render changes could silently break diagram types that have no snapshot coverage. The fidelity audit (2026-04-09) confirmed all types produce VALID SVG, but validity ≠ quality. Golden tests freeze "known good" output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- At least 12 new .mmd/.svg golden pairs added to crates/fm-cli/tests/golden/.
- `cargo test -p fm-cli --test golden_svg_test` passes with all new snapshots.
- Each SVG has been human-reviewed for reasonable visual quality.
- Any diagram types with clearly wrong output have follow-up beads filed.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:24:31.169977148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.269498405Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k3bv","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.268972699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

The golden test suite in crates/fm-cli/tests/golden/ has snapshots for core types (flowchart_simple, flowchart_cycle, class_basic, er_basic, c4_basic, all_node_shapes, all_edge_types, dense_flowchart_stress, empty_diagram) but is MISSING coverage for many diagram types that the README claims are "Implemented":
- kanban

- mindmap
- timeline

- block-beta
- sequence (basic exists but no advanced features)

## Scope
1. Create .mmd input files for each missing type with representative syntax covering the diagram's core features.
2. Generate initial .svg golden snapshots via `BLESS=1 cargo test -p fm-cli --test golden_svg_test`.
3. Manually review each SVG for reasonable visual quality (nodes positioned sensibly, edges routed, labels readable).
4. Document any types where the visual output is clearly wrong or significantly below expected quality — file separate beads for those fixes.

## Input file specifications (representative, non-trivial):
- **kanban**: 3 columns (Todo/In Progress/Done) with 2-3 cards each
- **packet-beta**: TCP header with 4+ fields spanning different bit ranges
- **architecture-beta**: 3 services with icons and 2 connections
- **requirementDiagram**: 2 requirements with a satisfaction relationship
- **journey**: 2 sections with 3+ tasks each, different satisfaction scores
- **gitGraph**: 3 branches, 2 merges, 1 cherry-pick
- **quadrantChart**: 4 quadrant labels, 4+ data points
- **xyChart**: 2 series (bar + line), 5+ data points
- **mindmap**: 3-level deep with different node shapes
- **timeline**: 4+ time periods with multiple events per period
- **sankey**: 5+ flows across 3 columns
- **pie**: 5 slices with percentages
- **stateDiagram**: composite state with 2 nested states + fork/join
- **block-beta**: 3-column grid with column spans and space blocks
- **sequence**: participants, messages, notes, alt fragment

## Why This Matters
Golden tests are the project's regression safety net. Without them, layout or render changes could silently break diagram types that have no snapshot coverage. The fidelity audit (2026-04-09) confirmed all types produce VALID SVG, but validity ≠ quality. Golden tests freeze "known good" output.

 {"id":"bd-keyp","title":"Flowchart: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Flowchart Feature Parity

## Current State
Flowchart is the most complete diagram type (~90% parser, ~85% SVG render). The core graph structure (nodes, edges, subgraphs, directions) works well. The gaps are in styling directives, interactive features, and constraint hints.

## Gaps to Close
1. classDef directive: define reusable style classes
2. style directive: apply inline styles to specific nodes
3. linkStyle directive: style specific edges
4. click directive rendering: make nodes clickable with URLs/callbacks
5. Tooltip support on hover
6. accDescription accessibility descriptions
7. Subgraph direction override (direction within subgraph)
8. Node markdown label support (using backtick syntax)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/flowchart.html
- Parser: crates/fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs lines 777-986
- SVG renderer: crates/fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:42:02.971032563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:19:41.813234263Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:19:41.813007033Z","close_reason":"All 8 feature gaps are now closed: classDef (bd-bfsm), style/linkStyle (bd-y6o8), click+tooltip (bd-peow), subgraph direction (bd-xwm3), markdown labels (bd-7ib6), parallel edge syntax (bd-bium). accDescription was already implemented via extract_accessibility_directives.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-7ib6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.904059282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bfsm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:37.663093628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-bium","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:30.829700237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-peow","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:39.308730074Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-xwm3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:40.108139881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-keyp","depends_on_id":"bd-y6o8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:28:38.499539334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-tvxc","title":"Class: Complete feature parity with mermaid-js","description":"# Class Diagram Feature Parity

## Current State
Class diagrams parse basic class declarations and relationships (~75% parser). The main gaps are member visibility modifiers, type annotations, generics, stereotypes, and namespace grouping.

## Gaps
1. Visibility modifiers (+, -, #, ~) on members/methods
2. Type annotations (returnType, parameterTypes)
3. Generic/template parameters (Class~T~)
4. Stereotypes (<<interface>>, <<abstract>>, <<enum>>)
5. Annotations and notes
6. Namespace grouping
7. Cardinality/multiplicity on relationship ends
8. Direction specification (class diagram direction)

## Reference
- mermaid-js docs: https://mermaid.js.org/syntax/classDiagram.html

 {"id":"bd-u4c1","title":"Replace hardcoded SVG band/axis colors with CSS custom properties","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded hex color strings in `render_layout_band()` and `render_layout_axis_tick()` functions in fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs with CSS custom property references.

## Why
These functions render gantt section labels (#4a5568), axis tick marks (#94a3b8), and axis tick labels (#64748b). These colors are theme-unaware — they are legible on the Default light theme but invisible on Dark, Neon, and Blueprint themes where the background is dark.

## Technical Context
These two functions (`render_layout_band` at line ~2050 and `render_layout_axis_tick` at line ~2104) only receive `&SvgRenderConfig` — they do NOT have access to the `Theme` struct. The options are:
1. Thread `&ThemeColors` through the function signatures (invasive refactor)
2. Use CSS custom properties like `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` (minimally invasive)

Option 2 is preferred because the theme CSS already defines these properties via `ThemeColors::to_css_vars()` in theme.rs, and SVGs with embedded `<style>` blocks will correctly resolve them.

## Specific Changes
- `render_layout_band()` line ~2095: Change `.fill("#4a5568")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #4a5568)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2112: Change `.stroke("#94a3b8")` to `.stroke("var(--fm-edge-color, #94a3b8)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2124: Change `.fill("#64748b")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #64748b)")`

## Status
PARTIALLY DONE — The CSS var approach was applied in the latest session. Verify the changes are committed and golden tests blessed.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Band labels use `var(--fm-text-color)` with appropriate fallback
- Axis ticks use `var(--fm-edge-color)` for stroke and `var(--fm-text-color)` for text
- All 10 theme presets produce readable axis labels in gantt/timeline diagrams
- Golden SVG snapshots blessed after change","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:03:40.755243683Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.042010891Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:00.041388102Z","close_reason":"Applied CSS var approach for band/axis colors in latest session","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-u785","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness","description":"## Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness

Build an automated visual regression testing harness that renders the complete FrankenMermaid test corpus to SVG/PNG, compares against golden reference images using perceptual diff, and produces a browsable HTML report. This serves dual purpose: regression detection AND an impressive demo of FrankenMermaid's rendering capabilities.

## Motivation
Layout algorithms are notoriously difficult to regression-test with unit tests alone. A pixel-level change in coordinate assignment can propagate to produce visually different (but structurally correct) layouts. Conversely, a bug might produce structurally incorrect output that looks "close enough" to pass manual review. The regression harness provides:
1. **Automated comparison:** perceptual diff between current and golden renders
2. **Browsable report:** HTML gallery showing before/after/diff for each test case
3. **Demo showcase:** the report itself IS the demo — browse all diagram types, see FrankenMermaid's quality
4. **Regression budget:** configure acceptable diff threshold (e.g., 0.1% pixel difference) per diagram type

## Perceptual Diff Algorithm
Use structural similarity index (SSIM) or perceptual hash (pHash) rather than pixel-exact comparison:
- **SSIM:** Compares luminance, contrast, and structure. Score from 0 to 1, where 1 = identical.
  SSIM(x,y) = [l(x,y)]^α * [c(x,y)]^β * [s(x,y)]^γ
  where l = luminance, c = contrast, s = structure comparison
- **pHash:** DCT-based perceptual hash. Hamming distance between hashes measures visual similarity.
- **Threshold:** SSIM > 0.99 = no regression, 0.95-0.99 = warning, < 0.95 = regression

## Implementation
1. Create `regression-harness/` binary crate in workspace.
2. Input: directory of .mmd files (test corpus) + golden reference renders.
3. Pipeline per file: parse → layout → render to SVG → rasterize to PNG (via resvg or similar).
4. Compare each PNG against golden reference using SSIM.
5. Generate HTML report: thumbnail grid, click to expand, before/after/diff overlay.
6. Golden reference update: `cargo run --bin regression-harness -- --update-goldens`.
7. CI integration: run on every PR that touches fm-layout or fm-render, fail if any SSIM < threshold.
8. Include timing data per render in the report (performance regression detection).

## Demo Aspect
The regression report doubles as the project's visual showcase:
- Categories: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, gantt charts, etc.
- Each category shows 5-10 representative diagrams at various complexities.
- Report includes metadata: node count, edge count, layout time, render time.
- Hosted version can be published to frankenmermaid.com/gallery for public demo.

## Success Metrics
- All diagram types in test corpus rendered and compared automatically
- SSIM threshold tuned to catch real regressions while ignoring sub-pixel jitter
- HTML report loads in < 2 seconds with 500+ thumbnails
- Golden update workflow is one command + explicit commit
- CI gate blocks merge on regression (SSIM < 0.95)
- Report generation completes in < 5 minutes for full corpus

## Dependencies
- Requires fm-render-svg (bd-1y5) for SVG output
- Requires test corpus (bd-2xl.1 golden test infrastructure)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:39.374613334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947294342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","layout","regression","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947218691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.324472620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.427880609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-udou","title":"Fix DOT body extraction with comment braces","description":"extract_body() uses first '{' in raw input; if comments contain braces before the header, DOT body extraction includes header text and breaks parsing. Strip comments before extracting body and add tests for leading comment braces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T22:12:43.451672330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T22:34:30.175872382Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T22:34:30.175673084Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ulag","title":"Implement diagram-level title in IR and render across all diagram types","description":"# Diagram-Level Title Support

## What mermaid-js Supports
Many diagram types support a title directive:
\`\`\`
---
title: My Diagram Title
---
flowchart LR
  A --> B
\`\`\`
Or inline: \`title My Diagram\` in gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant.

The title renders as centered text above the entire diagram.

## Current State
- YAML front-matter title: NOT extracted from init config
- Inline 'title' directive: Parsed and SKIPPED in gantt, journey, pie, etc.
- MermaidDiagramMeta has NO title field
- SVG renderer does NOT render any diagram title

## Implementation Plan
1. **IR** (fm-core): Add title: Option<String> to MermaidDiagramMeta.
2. **Parser**: In front-matter parsing, extract title field. In diagram-specific parsers (gantt, journey, timeline, pie, xychart, quadrant), capture the title directive text and store in meta.title.
3. **SVG Renderer**: When meta.title is Some, render as centered <text> element above the diagram content. Adjust viewBox to accommodate title height.
4. **Tests**: YAML front-matter title, inline title in gantt, inline title in pie, no title (verify no empty text element), long title (verify wrapping or truncation).

## Impact
This is a high-visibility feature — users expect their diagram title to show up. Currently it silently vanishes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:52.006771142Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-24T05:33:17.573322578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T05:33:17.572874430Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fm-core","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":199,"issue_id":"bd-ulag","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T06:27:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-uwtl","title":"Add classDef styling diagnostic: emit warning when classDef is parsed but ignored","description":"## Problem (interim fix before full classDef wiring)
Until bd-65l0 (full classDef→SVG wiring) is complete, users who write classDef directives get ZERO feedback that their styles are being silently dropped. This interim bead adds a diagnostic warning so users at least KNOW their styles aren't working.

## Scope
1. In fm-parser (or fm-core validation), after parsing classDef directives, emit a Warning-level diagnostic:
   "classDef directive parsed but not yet applied to rendered output. Styling will use theme defaults."
2. Same for `style` directives on individual nodes.
3. Make the diagnostic machine-readable (category: Compatibility, rule_id: "classdef-not-applied").

## Why This Matters
Silent failure is the worst UX. Even "unsupported" is better than silently dropping user intent. This is a 30-minute fix that dramatically improves the user experience until full classDef support ships.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `fm-cli validate` on input with classDef shows a warning about unsupported styling.
- `fm-cli render` on input with classDef includes the diagnostic in verbose output.
- Test: input with classDef produces at least one Compatibility warning.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:25:20.344430316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:39:54.687067495Z","closed_at":"2026-04-10T02:39:54.686886322Z","close_reason":"Added rule_id 'style-directive-not-applied' to existing compatibility warning for classDef/style/linkStyle directives. Updated test to verify rule_id.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-uwtl","depends_on_id":"bd-5k51","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-09T20:28:04.870271228Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
